raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California is one of the 36 states where courts often deny requests for restraining orders due to the residency status of the survivor or the perpetrator.",
    "Senate Bill 554 clarifies by law that any individual has the right to request a restraining order in California if the perpetrator lives in California or if the incident occurred in the state.",
    "The law aims to protect survivors of domestic and sexual violence, regardless of their residency status.",
    "Current uncertainties in the legal guidelines lead to California courts dismissing or prohibiting restraining order petitions even when survivors have sought safety through relocation.",
    "This uncertainty may cause survivors to forfeit crucial healing resources like domestic violence and sexual assault clinics, making them vulnerable to further attacks.",
    "The bill allows California to join 14 other states with comprehensive restraining order laws."
  ]
}
```
